post.summary: Create a chat session from a training job
post.description:
Creates a chat session bound to the training job's stack in one call.
Centralizes tenant/auth checks, job-ready validation, and session creation.

**Permissions:** Requires `admin` role (highest privilege level).

**Security:**
- Commands are validated against a strict whitelist
- Shell metacharacters are rejected (pipes, redirects, semicolons, etc.)
- All executions are audit logged

**Audit Events:**
- Success: `cli.owner_run` with command details
- Failure: `cli.owner_run` with error message

# Example
```json
POST /v1/cli/owner-run
{
  "command": "aosctl status",
  "session_id": "session-123"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "stdout": "Status: Running\nVersion: 0.3-alpha\n",
  "stderr": "",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "duration_ms": 42
}
```

post.summary: Upload files to create a new dataset
Initiate a chunked upload for files > 10MB

delete.summary: Cancel and cleanup an upload session
delete.description:
Cancels an ongoing chunked upload and removes all temporary files.
Use this if the client decides to abort an upload.

post.summary: Upload a single chunk for a chunked upload session
post.description:
This endpoint receives a single chunk of data for an ongoing chunked upload.
Chunks can be uploaded in any order and the system will track which chunks
have been received. The session must have been initiated first with the
initiate_chunked_upload endpoint.

## Error Cases
- 404: Session not found or expired (sessions expire after 24 hours)
- 400: Invalid chunk index (negative or exceeds expected chunks)
- 409: Chunk already uploaded (duplicate chunk index)
- 413: Chunk size exceeds the session's configured chunk size
- 500: Failed to write chunk to disk

post.summary: Complete a chunked upload and create the dataset
post.description:
This endpoint assembles all uploaded chunks into the final file and creates
a dataset entry in the database. All chunks must have been uploaded before
calling this endpoint.

## Cleanup Strategy
- On success: Temporary chunk files are deleted during assembly
- On failure: Temporary files remain for retry; session expires after 24 hours
- Abandoned sessions: Background cleanup runs every hour to remove expired sessions
  and their temporary files (see UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS in chunked_upload.rs)

400.description: Upload not complete or validation failed

get.summary: Get the status of an upload session
get.description:
Returns information about an ongoing chunked upload session, including
which chunks have been received and whether the upload is complete.

get.summary: Stream dataset upload and processing progress via SSE
get.description:
This endpoint establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection that streams
progress events for dataset operations. Clients can connect to receive real-time
updates about:
- File upload progress (percentage, current file)
- Dataset validation progress (files processed, validation results)
- Statistics computation progress

Events are JSON objects with the following fields:
- `dataset_id`: The ID of the dataset being processed
- `event_type`: One of "upload", "validation", or "statistics"
- `current_file`: The file currently being processed (optional)
- `percentage_complete`: Overall progress as a percentage (0-100)
- `total_files`: Total number of files in the dataset (optional)
- `files_processed`: Number of files processed so far (optional)
- `message`: Human-readable status message
- `timestamp`: RFC3339 formatted timestamp

Example client usage (JavaScript):
```javascript
const eventSource = new EventSource('/v1/datasets/upload/progress?dataset_id=abc123');
eventSource.onmessage = (event) => {
  const progress = JSON.parse(event.data);
  console.log(`${progress.message}: ${progress.percentage_complete}%`);
};
```

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of dataset progress

post.description:
Accepts inference requests and returns a stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE)
with tokens as they are generated. Compatible with OpenAI's streaming API.

# SSE Event Format
```text
data: {"id":"chatcmpl-xxx","object":"chat.completion.chunk","choices":[{"delta":{"content":"token"}}]}
data: [DONE]
```

# Collection-Scoped RAG
When `collection_id` is provided, the inference will be scoped to documents
within that collection, enabling targeted RAG retrieval for domain-specific queries.

# Example
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Hello, world!", "max_tokens": 100}'
```

# Example with Collection-Scoped RAG
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "What is the licensing model?", "max_tokens": 200, "collection_id": "marketing-docs"}'
```

post.summary: Streaming inference handler with loading progress
post.description:
Accepts inference requests and returns a stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE)
with model loading progress, then inference tokens. This endpoint automatically
detects if the requested adapter needs to be loaded and streams progress events.

# SSE Event Format
```text
data: {"event":"Loading","phase":"LoadingWeights","progress":0,"eta_seconds":30}
data: {"event":"Loading","phase":"Warmup","progress":50,"eta_seconds":10}
data: {"event":"Ready","warmup_latency_ms":1234}
data: {"event":"Token","text":"Hello","token_id":null}
data: {"event":"Done","total_tokens":10,"latency_ms":5678}
```

# Example
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream/progress \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Hello, world!", "max_tokens": 100, "adapters": ["my-adapter"]}'
```

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream with loading progress and tokens

get.summary: Get journey details by type and ID

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
All authenticated users

# Response
Returns a list of all models with associated statistics:
- `models`: Array of model records with:
  - `id`: Unique model identifier
  - `name`: Model name
  - `format`: Model format (mlx, safetensors, pytorch, gguf)
  - `backend`: Backend type (mlx-ffi, metal)
  - `size_bytes`: Total size of model files
  - `import_status`: Import status (available, in_progress, failed)
  - `model_path`: Filesystem path to model files
  - `capabilities`: List of supported capabilities
  - `adapter_count`: Number of adapters using this model
  - `training_job_count`: Number of training jobs for this model
  - `imported_at`: Import timestamp
  - `updated_at`: Last update timestamp
- `total`: Total number of models

# Errors
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure

# Example
```
GET /v1/models
```

post.summary: Import a model from a path on disk
post.description:
# Endpoint
POST /v1/models/import

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
Requires one of: Admin, Operator

# Request Body
- `model_name`: Name for the imported model
- `model_path`: Filesystem path to model directory
- `format`: Model format (mlx, safetensors, pytorch, gguf)
- `backend`: Backend to use (mlx-ffi, metal)
- `capabilities`: Optional list of capabilities (chat, completion, embeddings)
- `metadata`: Optional JSON metadata

# Response
Returns import status with:
- `import_id`: Unique identifier for the imported model
- `status`: Import status (available, in_progress, failed)
- `message`: Human-readable status message
- `progress`: Import progress percentage (0-100)

The import process:
1. Validates path exists and format is supported
2. Scans directory and computes BLAKE3 hashes for all files
3. Detects model structure and configuration
4. Registers model in database with metadata

# Errors
- `FORBIDDEN` (403): User lacks required role
- `BAD_REQUEST` (400): Invalid path, unsupported format, or unsupported backend
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Import failure, database error

# Example
```
POST /v1/models/import
{
  "model_name": "qwen-7b",
  "model_path": "/var/model-cache/models/qwen2.5-7b-instruct-bf16",
  "format": "mlx",
  "backend": "mlx-ffi",
  "capabilities": ["chat", "completion"]
}
```

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/status

# Authentication
Optional - unauthenticated requests receive limited response

# Permissions (when authenticated)
Requires one of: Operator, Admin, Compliance

# Query Parameters
- `tenant_id`: Optional tenant ID filter (defaults to "default", only applies when authenticated)

# Response
Returns the current base model load status. Response varies by authentication:

**Unauthenticated response (limited data):**
- `model_id`: "none"
- `model_name`: "No Model Loaded"
- `model_path`: null
- `status`: "unloaded"
- `loaded_at`: null
- `unloaded_at`: null
- `error_message`: null
- `memory_usage_mb`: null
- `is_loaded`: false
- `updated_at`: Current timestamp

**Authenticated response (full data):**
- `model_id`: Identifier of the loaded model (or "none")
- `model_name`: Human-readable model name (or "No Model Loaded")
- `model_path`: Filesystem path to model files
- `status`: Load status (loaded, unloaded, loading, error)
- `loaded_at`: Timestamp when model was loaded
- `unloaded_at`: Timestamp when model was unloaded
- `error_message`: Error message if status is error
- `memory_usage_mb`: Memory consumption in MB
- `is_loaded`: Boolean flag indicating if model is currently in memory
- `updated_at`: Last status update timestamp

# Errors
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model referenced in status record not found in database (authenticated only)
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure (authenticated only)

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/status?tenant_id=default
```

post.summary: Load a base model into memory
post.description:
# Endpoint
POST /v1/models/{model_id}/load

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
Requires one of: Admin, Operator

# Response
Returns current model load status including memory usage and load timestamp.
If model is already loaded, returns success with current status.

# Errors
- `FORBIDDEN` (403): User lacks required role
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model does not exist in database
- `WORKER_UNAVAILABLE` (503): No worker available to load the model
- `WORKER_ERROR` (500): Worker failed to load model
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database error, memory pressure, load failure

# Example
```
POST /v1/models/qwen-7b/load
```

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/{model_id}/status

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
All authenticated users

# Response
Returns the current load status of a model, including:
- `model_id`: Model identifier
- `model_name`: Human-readable model name
- `model_path`: Filesystem path to model files
- `status`: Load status (loaded, unloaded, loading, error)
- `loaded_at`: Timestamp when model was loaded (if loaded)
- `memory_usage_mb`: Memory consumption in MB (if loaded)
- `is_loaded`: Boolean flag indicating if model is currently in memory

# Errors
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model does not exist in database
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/qwen-7b/status
```

post.summary: Unload a base model from memory
post.description:
# Endpoint
POST /v1/models/{model_id}/unload

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
Requires one of: Admin, Operator

# Response
Returns updated model status confirming the model is unloaded. Frees GPU/memory resources.

# Errors
- `FORBIDDEN` (403): User lacks required role
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model does not exist in database
- `BAD_REQUEST` (400): Model not currently loaded
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database error, unload failure

# Example
```
POST /v1/models/qwen-7b/unload
```

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/{model_id}/validate

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
All authenticated users

# Response
Validates model integrity by checking stored BLAKE3 hashes. Returns:
- `model_id`: Model identifier
- `status`: Validation status (ready, invalid)
- `valid`: Boolean indicating if all hashes are valid
- `can_load`: Boolean indicating if model can be loaded
- `reason`: Description of validation failure (if any)
- `issues`: List of validation issues with type and message
- `errors`: Legacy field for backwards compatibility

Validates:
- Model weights file hash (BLAKE3)
- Config file hash (BLAKE3)
- Tokenizer files hashes (BLAKE3)
- Metadata JSON format

This is a logical validation (hash comparison) and does not require actual file access.

# Errors
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model does not exist in database
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/qwen-7b/validate
```

get.description:
Returns the currently active execution policy. If no explicit policy
is configured, returns a permissive default policy (marked with is_implicit=true).

# Errors
- 401: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication
- 403: Forbidden - tenant isolation violation
- 500: Internal server error

post.summary: Create a new execution policy for a tenant
post.description:
Creates a new policy version, deactivating any existing active policy.
Policy versions are tracked for audit trail.

# Errors
- 400: Bad Request - invalid policy configuration
- 401: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication
- 403: Forbidden - tenant isolation violation
- 500: Internal server error

get.summary: Get execution policy history for a tenant
get.description:
Returns all policy versions (active and inactive) ordered by version
descending (most recent first).

# Errors
- 401: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication
- 403: Forbidden - tenant isolation violation
- 500: Internal server error

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of policies to return (default: 10)

delete.description:
Deactivates the specified policy. The tenant will revert to the next
most recent active policy, or the permissive default if none exists.

# Errors
- 401: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication
- 403: Forbidden - tenant isolation violation
- 404: Not Found - policy not found or already inactive
- 500: Internal server error

AdapterLifecycleState.description:
Adapter lifecycle state

Mirrors the internal AdapterState enum from adapteros-lora-lifecycle

AdapterMemorySummary.description: Adapter memory summary for top-N display

externally_created.description: Flag indicating if this is an externally-created adapter (no training job)

oneOf.description: Training job that created this adapter (if any)

AdapterResponse.description:
Adapter response

# State Fields

This type exposes two distinct state concepts:

- `lifecycle_state`: The adapter's lifecycle phase in the release workflow.
  Values: "draft", "active", "deprecated", "retired"
  This is the **canonical field** for adapter maturity/release status.

- `runtime_state`: The adapter's current memory/runtime status.
  Values: "unloaded", "cold", "warm", "hot", "resident"
  This reflects whether the adapter is loaded and at what priority tier.

Note: In the database, `current_state` maps to `runtime_state` in the API
to provide clearer semantics.

deduplicated.description: Whether the adapter was deduplicated (found existing instead of creating new)

lifecycle_state.description:
Lifecycle state from migration 0068 (draft/active/deprecated/retired)
This is the canonical field for adapter maturity/release status.

lora_scope.description: Logical scope for routing (may mirror scope)

lora_strength.description: Runtime strength multiplier (scales LoRA application without changing alpha)

pinned.description: Whether adapter is pinned (protected from eviction)

runtime_state.description:
Runtime state indicating memory/load status (unloaded/cold/warm/hot/resident)
Maps from database `current_state` field.

version.description: Adapter version from migration 0068 (semantic or monotonic)

selected_count.description: Number of times selected by router

last_access.description: RFC3339 timestamp of last access (if known)

dry_run.description: If true, only validate the swap without executing

new_adapter_id.description: ID of the adapter to load

old_adapter_id.description: ID of the adapter to replace

dry_run.description: Whether this was a dry run

duration_ms.description: Duration of the swap operation in milliseconds

new_adapter_id.description: New adapter ID that was loaded

old_adapter_id.description: Old adapter ID that was replaced

AddChatMessageRequest.description: Request to add a message to a session

AdjacencyEdge.description: Edge in the adjacency matrix from one cluster to the next.

ApiErrorBody.description: Standard error envelope returned by the API for all 4xx/5xx responses.

detail.description: Optional developer-facing detail (stack trace, context, etc.)

message.description: Human-readable message suitable for UI display

request_id.description: Correlation ID that matches the `x-request-id` header and server logs

AuditExtended.description: Extended audit record with before/after CPID

allowed_domains.description: Allowed email domains for registration (empty = all)

dev_bypass_allowed.description: Whether dev bypass is actually allowed (computed from config)

dev_token_enabled.description: Whether dev login bypass is enabled in config

max_login_attempts.description: Maximum failed login attempts before lockout

BackendKind.description:
Canonical backend selector used across AdapterOS (inference + training).

All user- and config-facing backend strings must parse through this type so
we have a single source of truth (and error messages) for CoreML, MLX,
Metal, CPU, and Auto detection. CoreML is treated as a first-class option
(including ANE) and `Auto` preserves the current behavior of "pick the best
available backend" without changing defaults for existing callers.

id.description: Client-provided identifier used to correlate responses

BatchInferItemRequest.description: Single request item within a batch inference call

BatchInferItemResponse.description: Batch inference response item containing either a result or error

oneOf.description: Error information if the request failed

id.description: Identifier corresponding to the original request

requests.description: Collection of inference requests to run together

error.description: Error message if the item failed

total_items.description: Total number of items in the batch

BoundingBox.description: Bounding box for visual citations (e.g., PDF coordinates)

BranchClassification.description: Classification for adapter branches controlling promotion safeguards.

BrokenLink.description: Broken link in the audit chain

CategoryPolicyRequest.description: Category policy request for creating or updating

auto_demote.description: Whether to auto-demote based on inactivity

auto_promote.description: Whether to auto-promote based on usage

demotion_threshold_secs.description: Maximum time before demotion in seconds

eviction_priority.description: Eviction priority (never, low, normal, high, critical)

max_in_memory.description: Maximum number of adapters of this category to keep in memory

memory_limit.description: Memory limit in bytes for this category

promotion_threshold_secs.description: Minimum time before promotion in seconds

auto_demote.description: Whether to auto-demote based on inactivity

auto_promote.description: Whether to auto-promote based on usage

demotion_threshold_ms.description: Maximum time before demotion in milliseconds

max_in_memory.description: Maximum number of adapters to keep in memory

promotion_threshold_ms.description: Minimum time before promotion in milliseconds

ChainVerificationSchema.description: API schema version of chain verification (avoids pulling utoipa into DB types)

CharRange.description: Character range for precise text location

ChatBootstrapResponse.description:
Response for GET /v1/training/jobs/{id}/chat_bootstrap

Returns the "recipe" for starting a chat from a completed training job.
Used by any UI flow to quickly get the payload needed to create a chat session.

adapter_id.description: Primary adapter ID from training job (set after training completes)

adapter_version_id.description: Adapter version ID for display (e.g., adapter@version)

base_model.description: Base model ID used for training

collection_id.description: RAG collection ID if training involved RAG

dataset_version_id.description: Dataset version ID for citation scoping (immutable snapshot)

ready.description: Whether the training job is ready for chat (completed with stack)

stack_id.description: Stack ID created from training (if ready)

suggested_chat_title.description: Suggested title for the chat session

training_job_id.description: Training job ID (always present, echoed from path)

ChatProvenanceEntry.description: Single provenance entry captured for a chat inference call

ChatProvenanceResponse.description: Complete provenance graph for a chat session

oneOf.description: The base model used (derived from training jobs)

entries.description: Optional provenance entries captured per inference call

provenance_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of the provenance graph (for audit trail)

pinned_adapter_ids.description: Pinned adapter IDs for this session (JSON array)

citation_id.description: Deterministic BLAKE3 hash of citation content

offset_end.description: Byte offset where the chunk ends

offset_start.description: Byte offset where the chunk starts

preview.description: Short preview of the chunk text

rank.description: Rank of this citation in the result set

session_id.description: Optional session ID for tracking related commands

ClusterDefinition.description: Cluster definition within the semantic topology.

CompleteChunkedUploadRequest.description: Request to complete a chunked upload and create the dataset

name.description: Dataset name (optional, defaults to file name)

CompleteChunkedUploadResponse.description: Response from completing a chunked upload

hash.description: Final BLAKE3 hash of assembled file

CoreMLMode.description: CoreML execution mode for inference/training backend selection.

CreateActivityEventRequest.description: Request body for creating an activity event

metadata_json.description: Optional JSON metadata for additional event context

workspace_id.description: Optional workspace ID to associate with the event

CreateBatchJobRequest.description: Create batch job request (async batch processing)

max_concurrent.description: Optional maximum number of concurrent requests to process

requests.description: Collection of inference requests to process asynchronously

timeout_secs.description: Optional timeout in seconds for the entire batch job

CreateChatFromJobRequest.description:
Request for POST /v1/chats/from_training_job

Creates a chat session bound to a training job's stack in one call.

metadata_json.description: Optional metadata JSON for the chat session

name.description: Optional override for chat session name

training_job_id.description: Training job ID to create chat from

adapter_id.description: Primary adapter ID from the training job

stack_id.description: Stack ID the session is bound to

training_job_id.description: Training job ID (echoed from request for confirmation)

CreateChatSessionRequest.description: Request to create a new chat session

CreateEvidenceRequest.description: Request to create an evidence entry

adapter_id.description: Adapter ID (optional, must have dataset_id or adapter_id)

dataset_id.description: Dataset ID (optional, must have dataset_id or adapter_id)

evidence_type.description: Evidence type (doc, ticket, commit, policy_approval, data_agreement, review, audit, other)

reference.description: Reference (URL, commit SHA, ticket ID, document path)

CreateExecutionPolicyRequest.description: Request to create or update an execution policy

require_signed_adapters.description:
PRD-ART-01: Require Ed25519 signatures on imported .aos adapter files.
Default: false (unsigned adapters allowed)

CreateStackRequest.description: Request to create a new adapter stack

determinism_mode.description:
Determinism mode for this stack (strict, besteffort, relaxed)
If not specified, inherits tenant execution policy default when set,
otherwise falls back to the global default determinism mode (strict by default).

routing_determinism_mode.description: Routing determinism mode for this stack (deterministic/adaptive)

DashboardWidgetConfig.description: Dashboard widget configuration for a single widget

DataLineageMode.description: Lineage quality for training data provenance.

dataset_version_id.description: Latest trusted dataset version (effective trust applied; may be None if no trusted versions)

trust_state.description: Effective trust_state for the selected dataset_version_id (allowed/allowed_with_warning/blocked/needs_approval)

DatasetTrustOverrideRequest.description: Request to apply an admin trust override to a dataset version

override_state.description: Override state: allowed | allowed_with_warning | blocked | needs_approval

DatasetVersionSelection.description: Dataset version selector with optional sampling weight (API surface).

DatasetVersionSummary.description: Summary of a dataset version (used for dataset detail views and selectors)

trust_state.description: Effective trust_state for this version (includes overrides)

DatasetVersionTrustSnapshot.description: Trust snapshot for a dataset version captured at training time.

DeterminismPolicy.description:
Determinism policy configuration

Controls which determinism modes are allowed for inference requests
and how strict mode constraints are enforced.

allow_fallback.description:
Whether backend fallback is allowed.
When false, inference will fail rather than fall back to a different backend.

allowed_backends.description:
Optional allowlist of permitted backends for inference.
When present, requests for other backends will be rejected.

allowed_modes.description:
Allowed determinism modes for this tenant.
Valid values: "strict", "besteffort", "relaxed"
Empty array means all modes are allowed (permissive default).

default_mode.description:
Default mode when not specified in inference request.
Must be one of the allowed_modes (or any mode if allowed_modes is empty).

denied_backends.description: Optional denylist of backends; takes precedence over allowlist.

replay_mode.description:
Expected replay guarantee level: "exact", "approximate", or "none".
Used for documentation and validation, does not directly affect inference.

require_seed.description:
Whether seed is required in strict mode.
When true and mode is "strict", inference requests without seed will be rejected.

backend_used.description: Backend used to execute the inference (e.g., coreml, metal, mlx).

model.description: Model identifier used for this inference (base model).

prompt_system_params_digest_b3.description: BLAKE3 digest of (prompt + system + params), hex encoded.

router_seed.description: Router seed (audit only; routing is deterministic by algorithm).

sampling_params.description: Sampling parameters applied for token generation.

stack_id.description: Stack identifier used to scope adapter routing (if any).

deduplicated.description: True if this response points to a pre-existing document with identical content

FailureCode.description: Structured failure codes for smoke-test visibility and UI surfacing.

peers_out_of_sync.description: Number of peers out of sync

syncing.description: Whether the system is currently syncing

FusionIntervalTrace.description: Fusion interval boundary with fused tensor hash evidence

GetDashboardConfigResponse.description: Response containing all widget configurations for a user

golden.description: Name of the golden baseline to compare against

GoldenPolicy.description:
Golden-run verification policy configuration

Controls whether and how routing decisions are verified against
a golden baseline for drift detection.

epsilon_threshold.description:
Epsilon threshold for floating-point comparison of gate values.
Gate differences within this threshold are not considered drift.
Note: For CI, adapter selection/order changes always fail regardless of epsilon.

fail_on_drift.description:
Whether to fail inference when golden drift is detected.
When false (default), drift is logged but inference proceeds.
When true, inference is rejected on drift detection.

golden_baseline_id.description:
Golden baseline ID to compare against.
When None/null, golden verification is disabled.

adapters.description: List of adapters used in the run

has_signature.description: Whether the archive has a signature

layer_count.description: Number of layers in epsilon stats

max_epsilon.description: Maximum epsilon value across all layers

mean_epsilon.description: Mean epsilon value across all layers

GpuIntegrityReport.description:
GPU integrity verification report

Returned by external verification code to indicate which adapters passed/failed
GPU buffer integrity checks.

failed.description: Adapters that failed verification (id, name, reason)

skipped.description: Adapters that were skipped (not in GPU)

adapter_stack.description:
Explicit adapter list to use for inference (legacy field)

Historically used as a "stack" placeholder, this now represents a concrete
adapter list when provided. Prefer `stack_id` for named stacks.

adapters.description: Specific adapters to use (alternative to adapter_stack)

collection_id.description: Collection ID for scoped RAG retrieval (requires rag_enabled = true)

dataset_version_id.description:
Dataset version ID for deterministic dataset pinning

When provided, the inference is scoped to this exact dataset version,
enabling deterministic replay and explicit dataset pinning in receipts.

domain.description: Optional domain hint to bias package/adapters

effective_adapter_ids.description: Effective adapter set computed by the control plane (debug/audit only)

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate (fallbacks to manifest defaults).

prompt.description: Raw prompt text or chat payload.

rag_enabled.description: Enable RAG context retrieval for this inference request

session_id.description: Chat session ID for trace linkage

stack_id.description: Adapter stack identifier to use for inference

oneOf.description:
Stop policy specification for deterministic stop behavior

If not provided, a default policy is constructed from max_tokens.

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID (usually extracted from JWT claims, but can be explicit)

adapters_used.description: Adapters used for this inference (also available in trace)

backend_used.description: Backend used to execute the request (e.g., coreml, metal, mlx)

coreml_compute_units.description: CoreML compute units actually used (if applicable)

coreml_gpu_used.description: Whether CoreML leveraged GPU for this inference (if applicable)

determinism_mode_applied.description: Determinism mode that was applied after resolution

oneOf.description:
Deterministic receipt for audit/replay metadata.

Includes seeds, resolved parameters, and execution selection. This is
intended to be deterministic given the same prompt+system+params and
runtime state.

fallback_backend.description: Backend selected after fallback (if different from requested)

fallback_triggered.description: Whether backend fallback occurred during execution

latency_ms.description: Latency in milliseconds (also available in trace)

pinned_routing_fallback.description:
Routing fallback mode when pinned adapters are unavailable

- `None`: All pinned adapters were available (or no pins configured)
- `Some("partial")`: Some pinned adapters unavailable, using available pins + stack
- `Some("stack_only")`: All pinned adapters unavailable, routing uses stack only

oneOf.description: Replay guarantee level for this inference
Canonical run envelope describing the execution context.

stop_policy_digest_b3.description: BLAKE3 digest of the StopPolicySpec used (hex encoded)

oneOf.description: Stop reason code explaining why generation terminated

stop_reason_token_index.description: Token index at which the stop decision was made

unavailable_pinned_adapters.description:
Pinned adapters that were unavailable for this inference

These are adapters that were in the session's pinned set but were not
available in the candidate adapter set. Returned for UI warning display.

rag_collection_id.description: Collection ID used for scoped RAG retrieval

rag_doc_ids.description: JSON array of document IDs in retrieval order (aggregate RAG trace)

rag_scores.description: JSON array of relevance scores parallel to rag_doc_ids

active_experts.description: Flattened expert IDs per token for quick visualization

expert_routing.description: Per-token expert routing data (layer_idx, expert_id) for MoE models

fusion_intervals.description: Fusion intervals and fused tensor hashes for determinism evidence

oneOf.description: Model type for the trace (dense vs MoE)
MoE metadata for the underlying model (if applicable)

IngestRouterDecisionRequest.description: Request to ingest a router decision event (internal endpoint)

InitiateChunkedUploadRequest.description: Request to initiate a chunked upload

chunk_size.description: Chunk size preference (will be clamped to valid range)

InitiateChunkedUploadResponse.description: Response from initiating a chunked upload

chunk_size.description: Chunk size that will be used

IntegrityStatus.description: Integrity posture for the control plane.

KernelMemorySummary.description: Memory summary for UMA and ANE where available.

adapters.description: Number of adapters in KV (if available)

stacks.description: Number of stacks in KV (if available)

tenants.description: Number of tenants in KV (if available)

users.description: Number of users in KV (if available)

evidence.description: Lightweight evidence blob (JSON) for audit attachment.

oneOf.description: KV entry refers to a different tenant than SQL ground truth.
KV entry exists but SQL does not.
SQL entry exists but KV does not.
Field differs between SQL and KV for the same id.
KV key prefix encodes a different tenant than the serialized value.

KvIsolationIssue.description: Type of issue detected during a scan.

ListSessionsParams.description: Query parameters for listing runtime sessions

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

dev_login_enabled.description: Whether dev login bypass is enabled

production_mode.description: Whether production mode is enabled in config

MfaDisableRequest.description: Disable MFA request (requires TOTP or backup code)

totp_code.description: First TOTP code to confirm the secret

experts_per_token.description: Number of experts activated per token

is_moe.description: Whether the underlying model is MoE

ModelLoadStatus.description:
Canonical base model load status used across API, worker, and UI.

Allowed transitions:
- no-model -> loading (load requested)
- loading -> ready (load success)
- loading -> error (load failure)
- loading -> no-model (cancel/timeout)
- ready -> unloading (unload/eviction requested)
- unloading -> no-model (unload success)
- unloading -> error (unload failure)
- error -> loading (retry/ensure)
- error -> no-model (reset/cleanup)

NodeDetailResponse.description: Node detail response with complete node information

ane_available.description: Whether ANE (Apple Neural Engine) is available

gpu_available.description: Whether GPU is available on this node

deduplicated.description: True if this response points to a pre-existing document with identical content

PolicyOverrideFlags.description: Flags describing which policy overrides affected routing.

create_stack.description:
Create a new stack with the adapter after registration (default: true).
Note: The new stack will NOT be set as the tenant's default stack.

register.description: Register adapter in registry after packaging (default: true)

tier.description: Tier to assign: persistent, warm, ephemeral (default: warm)

PredictedPathNode.description: Node prediction returned alongside the topology graph when context is provided.

adapter_id.description: Adapter identifier when this node represents an adapter.

cluster_id.description: Cluster the adapter belongs to (best-effort, optional).

confidence.description: Confidence or gate value produced by the router (0..1).

RagStatus.description: RAG system status indicating whether embedding model is available

ReceiptSamplingParams.description: Sampling parameters applied for inference execution (receipt).

seed.description: Random seed used for sampling (if any).

ReplayGuarantee.description: Replay guarantee level for an inference

RepoDetailResponse.description: Repository detail response with full metadata

RepoTier.description: Repository assurance tier used for backend defaults and promotion policy.

RouterCandidate.description: Candidate adapter entry for router trace

RouterCandidateResponse.description: Router candidate response with computed fields

RouterDecision.description: Router decision at a specific position (canonical schema)

active_experts.description: Active experts used for this token when running an MoE model

model_type.description: Model type for this decision (dense vs MoE)

RouterDecisionHash.description: Decision hash material for audit (mirrors router DecisionHash)

RouterModelType.description: Routing model type for trace display

RoutingDecision.description: Single routing decision with full candidate details

RoutingDecisionResponse.description: Routing decision response (enriched with parsed candidates)

RoutingDecisionsQuery.description: Routing decisions query parameters with comprehensive filters

source_type.description: Optional chat source_type filter; matched via chat_sessions on request_id

RoutingPolicy.description:
Routing policy configuration

Minimal, deterministic constraints applied after router scoring but
before kernels run. Designed to express per-tenant/workspace adapter
allowlists/denylists and an optional per-token adapter cap without
changing router math.

allowed_adapter_ids.description:
Restrict routing to specific adapter IDs.
When present, only these adapters may be routed.
When None/null, all adapters are allowed.

allowed_clusters.description:
Restrict routing to specific clusters (semantic grouping of adapters).
When present, only these clusters may be used.

allowed_stack_ids.description:
Restrict routing to specific stack IDs.
When present, only these stacks may be used.
When None/null, all stacks are allowed.

cluster_fallback.description:
Behavior when a cluster transition is blocked.
- stay_on_current: remain on current adapters
- fallback_to_base: route to base-only

denied_adapter_ids.description:
Explicitly deny routing to specific adapter IDs.
Takes precedence over allowlist when both are provided.

denied_clusters.description: Explicitly deny routing to specific clusters.

max_adapters_per_token.description:
Maximum number of adapters allowed per token after routing policy is applied.
When None/null, defaults to the router's K value.

max_reasoning_depth.description:
Maximum number of cluster transitions (hops) allowed per request.
When exceeded, router applies cluster_fallback behavior.

pin_enforcement.description:
How to handle pins outside the effective routing set.
"warn": Log warning but allow inference (default)
"error": Reject inference request

require_pins.description:
Require pinned adapters to be specified.
When true, requests without pinned_adapter_ids are rejected.

require_stack.description:
Require a stack to be specified in inference requests.
When true, requests without stack_id are rejected.

RunActor.description: Initiating actor for a run envelope.

auth_mode.description: Auth mode used for the request (bearer, api_key, dev_bypass, unauthenticated).

principal_type.description: Principal type (user, api_key, dev_bypass, internal_service, anonymous).

subject.description: Subject identifier (user id, dev-bypass, or anonymous).

RunEnvelope.description: Canonical execution context threaded through a run.

boot_trace_id.description: Boot trace identifier for the serving process.

determinism_version.description: Determinism version marker (legacy; use schema_version for schema compatibility).

manifest_hash_b3.description: BLAKE3 hash of the manifest used for this run.

policy_mask_digest_b3.description: Policy mask digest applied at the edge (BLAKE3 hex).

router_seed.description: Router seed reference for routing determinism.

schema_version.description: Schema version marker for envelope compatibility.

tick.description: Global tick assigned to this run (if available).

RunReceipt.description: Verifiable run receipt (hash chain over per-token decisions)

billed_input_tokens.description: Billed input tokens (logical - cached, floored at 0)

billed_output_tokens.description: Billed output tokens (v1 = logical output tokens)

kv_evictions.description: Number of KV cache evictions that occurred during inference

kv_quota_enforced.description: Whether KV quota enforcement was active for this inference

kv_residency_policy_id.description: ID of the residency policy governing KV cache management

logical_prompt_tokens.description: Total logical prompt tokens before cache reuse

model_cache_identity_v2_digest_b3.description:
BLAKE3 digest of ModelCacheIdentityV2 canonical bytes.
Binds the receipt to the exact kernel/quant/fusion/tokenizer/tenant/worker combination.

prefix_cache_hit.description: Whether the prefix KV cache was hit (true) or missed (false)

prefix_cached_token_count.description: Tokens satisfied by prefix cache reuse

prefix_kv_bytes.description: Bytes of cached KV tensors for the prefix

prefix_kv_key_b3.description: Cryptographic key for the prefix KV cache entry used

run_head_hash.description: Head hash for the run's trace chain.

stop_policy_digest_b3.description: BLAKE3 digest of the StopPolicySpec used for this inference

oneOf.description: Stop reason code explaining why generation terminated

stop_reason_token_index.description: Token index at which the stop decision was made

tenant_kv_bytes_used.description: Actual KV cache bytes used for this inference

tenant_kv_quota_bytes.description: Tenant's allocated KV cache quota in bytes

trace_id.description: Unique trace identifier for this run.

uptime_seconds.description: Uptime in seconds since session start

last_check.description: RFC3339 timestamp of last health check

determinism_mode.description:
Determinism mode for this stack (strict, besteffort, relaxed)
If not specified, uses global config

is_default.description: Whether this is the default stack for the tenant

lifecycle_state.description: Lifecycle state: active, deprecated, retired, draft

routing_determinism_mode.description: Routing determinism mode for adapter selection

warnings.description: Warnings about capacity or memory pressure (memory guardrails)

adapter_count.description: Number of adapters in this stack

adapters.description: Adapters in this stack (may be empty if include_adapters=false)

is_active.description: Whether this stack is currently active

api_patterns.description: API patterns to focus on (for framework adapters)

base_model_id.description: Base model ID for provenance tracking

base_version_id.description: Base adapter version ID (for finetuning an existing version)

category.description: Adapter category: code, framework, codebase, docs, domain

code_commit_sha.description: Code commit SHA when training is tied to source control

collection_id.description: Document collection ID for provenance tracking

data_spec.description: Data spec (DSL or JSON) used for this run

data_spec_hash.description: Canonical hash of the dataset manifest(s) used when the job was created

dataset_version_ids.description: Dataset versions to train on (with optional sampling weights)

exclude_patterns.description: File patterns to exclude (for codebase adapters)

file_patterns.description: File patterns to include (for codebase adapters)

lora_tier.description: Marketing/operational tier for routing and UI badges (micro/standard/max)

oneOf.description: Actions to perform after training completes

scope.description: Logical scope for adapter visibility (e.g., project, tenant)

synthetic_mode.description: Allow synthetic/diagnostic training data instead of datasets

target_branch.description: Target branch for the produced adapter version

StatusIndicator.description: Canonical status indicator for checks and overall readiness.

StopPolicySpec.description:
Stop policy specification for deterministic stopping behavior.

Configures thresholds and parameters for the stop controller.
The policy is hashed (BLAKE3) and committed to the receipt for audit.

completion_threshold_q15.description:
Q15 threshold for EOS probability triggering COMPLETION_CONFIDENT.
Range: 0-32767 where 32767 = 1.0 probability.
Default: 24576 (~0.75)

eos_token_id.description: Optional explicit EOS token ID (uses model default if not specified)

output_max_tokens.description: Hard cap on output tokens (overrides request max_tokens if lower)

repetition_ngram.description:
N-gram size for repetition detection (minimum 3).
Default: 3

repetition_window.description:
Sliding window size for repetition detection in tokens.
Default: 32

StopReasonCode.description: Exhaustive stop reason codes for inference termination.

StreamQuery.description: Stream query parameters (for training and contacts streams)

StreamingChoice.description: Individual choice in a streaming response

stop_policy_digest_b3.description: BLAKE3 digest of the StopPolicySpec used (hex encoded)

oneOf.description: Stop reason code explaining why generation terminated (PRD: Hard Deterministic Stop Controller)

stop_reason_token_index.description: Token index at which the stop decision was made

StreamingInferRequest.description:
Streaming inference request

Accepts the same fields as the standard `/v1/infer` endpoint but returns
a stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE) with tokens as they are generated.

adapter_stack.description: Adapter stack to use for inference

collection_id.description: Collection ID for scoping RAG retrieval to specific document collection

domain.description: Optional domain hint to bias adapter/package selection

effective_adapter_ids.description: Effective adapter IDs (control-plane computed; ignored from clients)

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

reasoning_mode.description: Enable reasoning-aware routing and mid-flight swaps

session_id.description: Session ID for linking inference to chat sessions

stack_id.description: Adapter stack identifier to use for inference

oneOf.description: Stop policy specification (PRD: Hard Deterministic Stop Controller)

SystemHealthResponse.description: Aggregate health response for all components

cpu_usage_percent.description: CPU usage as percentage (0-100), mirrors cpu_usage for frontend compatibility

memory_usage_percent.description: Memory usage as percentage (0-100), mirrors memory_usage for frontend compatibility

tokens_per_second.description: Tokens generated per second across all workers

SystemOverviewResponse.description: System overview response with complete system state

SystemStateQuery.description: Query parameters for system state endpoint

include_adapters.description: Include adapter details in stack responses (default: true)

tenant_id.description: Filter to specific tenant (Admin can see all, others see own)

top_adapters.description: Number of top adapters by memory to include (default: 10)

SystemStateResponse.description:
Ground truth system state response

Provides hierarchical visibility into the entire system state including:
- Node hardware and service health
- Tenants with their stacks and adapters
- Memory pressure and top adapters by usage

origin.description: Origin node that produced this data

oneOf.description: RAG status (whether embedding model is loaded and available)

tenants.description: Tenant states with nested stacks and adapters

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when this response was generated

inference_blockers.description: Reasons inference is blocked (empty when ready or unknown).

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when this snapshot was produced.

datasets.description: Number of training datasets in SQL

stacks.description: Number of adapter stacks in SQL

TenantExecutionPolicy.description:
Tenant execution policy

Hierarchical policy containing determinism, routing, and golden verification
configuration for a tenant. Loaded at inference time to enforce constraints.

active.description: Whether this policy is active (only one active policy per tenant)

is_implicit.description:
Whether this is an implicit permissive policy (no explicit policy configured)
When true, this policy was auto-generated as a permissive default.

require_signed_adapters.description:
PRD-ART-01: Require Ed25519 signatures on imported .aos adapter files.
When true, adapter imports without valid signatures will be rejected.
Default: false (unsigned adapters allowed)

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID this policy belongs to

updated_at.description: When the policy was last updated

version.description: Policy version for audit trail (increments on update)

adapter_count.description: Total number of adapters for this tenant

memory_usage_mb.description: Total memory usage across all adapters in MB

stacks.description: Stacks belonging to this tenant (includes active stack)

TenantSummary.description: Minimal tenant summary for tenant picker

TokenRevocationResponse.description: Response from tenant-wide token revocation operation

TopologyGraph.description: Complete topology graph returned by the API.

backend_policy.description: Backend policy when CoreML is preferred (coreml_only/coreml_else_fallback/auto)

coreml_placement.description: Optional CoreML placement spec for training/export alignment

coreml_training_fallback.description: Explicit fallback when CoreML is requested and unavailable

enable_coreml_export.description: Opt-in CoreML export after successful training

max_gpu_memory_mb.description: Maximum GPU memory in MB (best-effort, 0/unset = unlimited)

preferred_backend.description: Optional GPU backend preference (coreml, mlx, metal, cpu)

require_gpu.description: Require GPU acceleration (error if no GPU backend can be initialized)

base_model_id.description: Base model ID used for training

adapter_id.description: Adapter ID after packaging (populated on completion)

adapter_repo_id.description: Alias of `repo_id` for clarity in clients that treat this as an adapter repo identifier.

artifact_hash_b3.description: Alias of `package_hash_b3` for clients expecting an "artifact hash" surface.

artifact_path.description: Alias of `aos_path` for clients expecting an "artifact path" surface.

base_model_id.description: Base model ID used for training

collection_id.description: Document collection ID used for training

config_hash_b3.description: BLAKE3 hash of training config for reproducibility

dataset_hash_b3.description: Dataset manifest hash captured at job creation (combined when multi-dataset).

dataset_version_ids.description: Dataset versions used for this job (order preserved)

dataset_version_trust.description: Trust snapshot for dataset versions at training start

lora_tier.description: Marketing/operational tier for routing and UI badges (micro/standard/max)

scope.description: Logical scope for adapter visibility (e.g., project, tenant)

weights_hash_b3.description: BLAKE3 hash of adapter weights (for verification)

UpdateCollectionRequest.description: Request to update collection binding for a session

UpdateDashboardConfigRequest.description: Request to update dashboard widget configurations

UpdateDatasetSafetyRequest.description: Update semantic/safety statuses for a dataset version (Tier 2).

UploadChunkQuery.description: Query parameters for uploading a chunk

resume_token.description: Resume token for resuming from next chunk (if not complete)

UploadSessionStatusResponse.description: Response for getting upload session status

is_complete.description: Whether all chunks have been received

received_chunk_indices.description: List of chunk indices that have been received

active_adapter_ids.description: Adapters to keep active for this workspace (optional).

active_base_model_id.description: Base model to mark as active for this workspace (optional).

active_plan_id.description: Plan to mark as active for this workspace (optional).

manifest_hash_b3.description: Manifest hash associated with the active plan/model.
